questions.question: Population refers to:

options.A: Number of animals in a region
options.B: Number of people living in a particular area
options.C: Number of buildings in a city
options.D: Number of rivers in a country

questions.explanation: Population means the total number of people living in a particular area.

questions.question: India accounts for about what percentage of the world’s population?
questions.explanation: India has about 17% of the world’s population.

questions.question: What percentage of the world's land area does India occupy?
questions.explanation: India occupies about 2.4% of the world's land area.

questions.question: Which state is the most populous in India?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh is the most populous state in India.

questions.question: Population density means:

options.B: Number of people per square kilometre
options.C: Number of roads in a city
options.D: Number of rivers in a state

questions.explanation: Population density is the number of persons per square kilometre.

questions.question: India’s population density in 2011 was:
questions.explanation: India's population density in 2011 was 382 persons per square kilometre.

questions.question: Population growth refers to:
questions.explanation: Population growth means increase in the number of people.

questions.question: Birth rate refers to:

options.A: Number of births per thousand people per year

questions.explanation: Birth rate is the number of live births per thousand persons per year.

questions.question: Death rate refers to:

options.B: Number of deaths per thousand persons

questions.explanation: Death rate is the number of deaths per thousand persons in a year.

questions.question: Migration means:

options.B: Movement of people from one place to another

questions.explanation: Migration is the movement of people from one place to another.

questions.question: Internal migration means:
questions.explanation: Internal migration occurs within a country.

questions.question: International migration means:
questions.explanation: International migration occurs between different countries.

questions.question: Natural increase is calculated by:
questions.explanation: Natural increase is the difference between birth rate and death rate.

questions.question: Adolescents belong to which age group?
questions.explanation: Adolescents are people aged 10–19 years.

questions.question: Adolescents form approximately what fraction of India's population?
questions.explanation: Adolescents form about one-fifth of India's population.

questions.question: Urban population refers to:

options.B: People living in towns and cities

questions.explanation: Urban population includes people living in towns and cities.

questions.question: A million-plus city has a population of:
questions.explanation: Million-plus cities have population exceeding one million.

questions.question: Which factor attracts people to cities?
questions.explanation: Better employment opportunities attract migrants to cities.

questions.question: Which factor forces people to migrate from rural areas?
questions.explanation: Poverty and unemployment push people to leave rural areas.

questions.question: Anaemia is commonly found among:
questions.explanation: Anaemia is common among adolescent girls due to poor nutrition.

questions.question: National Population Policy was introduced in:
questions.explanation: National Population Policy was introduced in 2000.

questions.question: The main aim of population policy is:
questions.explanation: Population policy aims to control population growth.

questions.question: Which sector mainly attracts migrants to cities?
questions.explanation: Industries and services provide employment opportunities in cities.

questions.question: Population composition refers to:

options.B: Population structure by age, sex and occupation

questions.explanation: Population composition refers to population structure based on age, sex and occupation.

questions.question: Population planning helps:
questions.explanation: Population planning helps balance population growth with available resources.
